description_long: This is a large, weathered barn with a classic gambrel roof, featuring a prominent central section and two lower side extensions. The roof is covered in corrugated metal panels, showing signs of rust and age, giving it a rustic, utilitarian appearance. The exterior siding is vertically oriented and appears distressed, with visible wear and fading. The front facade has a large, rectangular main door and two smaller side openings, likely for equipment or livestock access. There are no visible windows, emphasizing its functional, agricultural design. The barn sits on a flat, rectangular base, suggesting it is intended to be placed on level ground.
description: A large, weathered barn with a gambrel roof, corrugated metal panels, and distressed vertical siding. The front features a large main door and two side openings, with no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main large door and two smaller side openings, with the gambrel roof and distressed vertical siding clearly visible.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the corrugated metal roof panels and the barn's overall rectangular footprint, with the central and side roof sections distinct.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, unadorned back wall with no doors or windows, and the full height of the gambrel roof.
description_view_3: Top-down view from the rear, emphasizing the roof's structure and the barn's overall massing without visible openings.
attribute_roof_type: gambrel roof with central and side extensions
attribute_door_style: single large rectangular hinged door with two smaller side openings
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any facade
